_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Procedure on Fallopian Tube for establishing Tubal Patency
clinical_indications: Reversal of tubal sterilization for want of child
Mild tubal block due to various pathology (e.g. endometriosis, past pelvic surgery, Pelvic inflammatory disease)
Tubal occlusion secondary to ectopic pregnancy treatment

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, indications, symptoms, signs, examination findings and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed clinical notes available with all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure and advice for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Is history with evidence (previous tubal ligation done - previous operative notes, indication / anatomical abnormality - HSG showing bilateral tubal block) or current imaging suggestive of diagnosis?
Is mid tubal block due to various pathology (e.g. endometriosis, past pelvic surgery, pelvic inflammatory disease) a documented indication for surgery?
Is tubal occlusion secondary to ectopic pregnancy treatment a documented indication for surgery?
Is salpingitis isthmica nodosa a documented indication for surgery?
Is there evidence of genital tuberculosis?
Is there evidence of sclerotic tubes or dense adhesions?
Is there evidence of bilateral fimbriectomy done?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge available?
Was the post-operative HSG report submitted?

common_queries: Detailed clinical notes for pre-authorization missing or incomplete, or planned line of treatment not documented.
Pre-authorization documentation missing HSG (hysterosalpingogram) or Sonosalpingography report.
Indication for surgery (mid tubal block due to pathology) not documented or without USG report.
Indication for surgery (tubal occlusion post ectopic pregnancy) not documented or without previous operative notes.
Indication for surgery (salpingitis isthmica nodosa) not documented or without HSG report.
Evidence of contraindications such as genital tuberculosis, sclerotic tubes/dense adhesions, or bilateral fimbriectomy found.
Claim submission missing detailed indoor case papers, procedure/operative notes, or discharge summary.
Post-operative HSG (hysterosalpingogram) report not submitted with the claim.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability
clinical_key_pointers: Tubal disease accounts for 25%- 35% of female factor infertility, with more than half of the cases due to salpingitis. In addition, large studies report that up to 20%-30% of women regret having a tubal ligation. Thus, there is a need to determine the optimal treatment methods for patients
surgical_options: Tubal cannulation for proximal tubal obstruction in young women with no other significant infertility factors.
Laparoscopic fimbrioplasty or neosalpingostomy for the treatment of mild hydrosalpinges in young women with no other significant infertility factors.
Laparoscopic salpingectomy or proximal tubal occlusion in cases of surgically irreparable hydrosalpinges to improve IVF pregnancy rates.
Microsurgical anastomosis for tubal ligation reversal.
